item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it. Rather an original take on time alteration (made me think of the retroannual plants in the Discworld series of books).
The e-mail exchanges were a bit long, but I don't mind because they allowed to flesh out the characters nicely and witness firsthand how the alteration took place.

One question though, I am surprised that the possibility of affecting the coyotes by altering documentation of the past is not specified in the containment procedures? You'd have thought the Foundation would have been doing some research about it and even documented it as a last measure in case of a containment breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to upvote it badly, and maybe I will, but two things keep me from doing so for now.

First, the exchange between the family is a tad too long. It isn't at all bad per se, and it is interesting to see how each character have quite distinct styles of writing. But sometimes it just drags a little, it could be shortened. Or maybe not, that is hardly the biggest problem.

What I really disliked was how B.B. solved the problem. Not that I have a dislike on the WWS employee being the one to come with the solution. But the solution was pure wishful thinking. He wished so hard for it to work that it worked. I like how his mom figured out the situation and the idea that he could try a creative solution based on her theory, but the actual solution found was a poor last minute choice.

The anomaly, as far as it could be understood, created the fake documentation. But nothing on it indicates that faking another documents could affect the anomaly. If some indication of this where present and recognized later, then his actions would make sense. On the other hand, he could try to convince the coyotes themselves that they where neutered in the past, or something like that, instead of just writing down in his personal calendar and wishing that to be true.

Even a little and hastily executed campaign of disinformation to try to convince other people of the truth of this alternative past would be an interesting solution, and indicate that there is still something else to be investigated in the future about the anomaly. Or he found something in his investigations of the breeding grounds that was an aftereffect of the "time alteration", or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The email exchange sold it for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: B.B. didn't have any wounds until after he neutered the coyotes; they manifested retroactively because he directly handled all of them with all the risks that come with that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really interesting concept of retroactivity, I like it a lot. One thing bothers me though, and I'm not sure if it was intentional or not. When B.B. set the precedent to spay and neuter all of the coyotes, that made all of the generated coyotes disappear, as well as the wounds they caused. Why then does B.B. still have his wounds?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who or what is Safari Joe? Where did he come from, and where did he go?
no but seriously im curious
EDIT- Apparently, his name was Safari DAVE. Making the above pun irrelevant and stupid. I was just being a dumb stupid idiot who is stupid and dumb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MAYBE THEYLL COME BACK FOR THE TURTLES SYLVESTER
WOULD YOU LIKE THEM TO TAKE THE TURTLES BACK

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is perhaps the least broken, and realistically manageable, version of retroactive powers I've seen in fiction. But even still, this could have gone Keter very easily if not for some quick creativity from WWS.

Anyway I really liked this skip, great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very satisfying to read. My favorite Wilson's skip so far, good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Acme was definitely intentional. B.B. and Sylvester wasn't but hah, funny coincidence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The gradual deterioration of the situation and that solution at the end all made for a fun and clever SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More remarking on how you've got coyotes, Acme, Sylvester and "B.B." That can't all be coincidental.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's right. Of course, no instance has been generated that's less than 4 years old.

Documents and evidence might claim they were younger at some point, but said evidence only comes into existence the same moment the coyotes do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a little unclear from the article. It states the coyotes appear two years younger then the mother. So if she was four, they'd be two and if she was six, they'd be four correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not Looney Tunes, just coyotes that happen to be associated with a very strange temporal anomaly of sorts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this just… Looney Tunes, the SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's my second SCP on the mainlist! It's about retroactive coyotes and the family that has to deal with them

Image is from Pixabay

I'd also like to thank Darkstuff and weryllium and the rest of the IRC channel for help and critique, couldn't get nearly as far without you guys!

Hope it's fun!
